name: Bingie Madison
abstract: Bingie Madison (October 12, 1901 – July 1978) was an American jazz clarinetist and tenor saxophonist. Madison began his career as a pianist, based in Des Moines, Iowa, and then touring Canada and California in 1921.

works.t: What Is This Thing Called Swing?

about_quotes.speaker: Monk Montgomery
about_quotes.evidence: And we had a band -- we used to broadcast from Hot Feet... Otto was the lead saxophone, I was second alto, Crawford was the third alto and Bingie -- three altos and one tenor.

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Coleman
about_quotes.evidence: Russell's band had changed musicians considerably... The sax section consisted of Glyn Pague and Henry 'Moon' Jones on altos, Bingie Madison tenor.

about_quotes.speaker: Snub Mosley
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Because he would have had--Well, Swampland was Bingie's tune, Bingie Madison; I know that. [INTERVIEWEE] Did Bingie write that?

about_quotes.speaker: Big Chielf Moore
about_quotes.evidence: I didn't know Bingie until I went to sit in over there, the Tango Palace, and I saw this fellow and we're talking about Louis Armstrong

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Durham
about_quotes.evidence: when I came to New York, that's when I run into guys like Bingie Madison
